frameworks.name: Bayesian observer model
frameworks.one_line: Perception as posterior combining slow-speed prior with sensory likelihood.

frameworks.name: Efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: Constrains sensory uncertainty structure so the Bayesian model is identifiable.

frameworks.name: Neural population coding
frameworks.one_line: Area MT tuning curves treated as the neural-level prior over speed.

core_question: Does a behavioral slow-speed prior inferred from psychophysics match the encoding accuracy of speed-tuned neurons in area MT?
key_assumptions: Observers combine a prior with a likelihood via Bayes rule
Sensory likelihood structure is constrained by efficient coding
MT population coding accuracy reflects an implicit neural prior over speed
manipulations: Comparison of behavioral prior (fit to psychophysics) vs neural prior (from MT population)
Cross-validation model comparison between the two priors
key_findings: Efficient-coding-constrained Bayesian observer explains human visual speed perception
MT population coding accuracy matches a power-law slow-speed prior
Behavioral and neural priors are indistinguishable in cross-validation
interpretations: The Bayesian observer model under efficient coding is quantitatively validated at both behavioral and neural levels
